summary: The capsule correctly captures the heap-pressure-to-OOM-kill causal chain and excludes the chronyd distractor, but fails to parse __REALTIME_TIMESTAMP epoch values so the window is 'unknown', and the systemd 'A process has been killed' message is downgraded to 'context' rather than 'consequence'.
improvements: Parse __REALTIME_TIMESTAMP (microseconds since epoch) to a human-readable UTC time so the window field is populated and timestamps are interpretable without a separate conversion step

Relabel line 10 (systemd 'A process of this unit has been killed by the OOM killer') as consequence rather than context — it is a direct result of the kernel kill and confirms the service-level impact
